report_name: ShinyHunters Claims 4.9M Charter Communications Accounts Stolen via Vishing Attack

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A notorious extortion group that claimed responsibility for the Charter Communications data breach. They allegedly initiated the breach via a vishing attack to compromise a Microsoft Entra account, which was then used to access a Salesforce instance and exfiltrate customer data.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: Used to deceive a Charter Communications employee into providing information that led to the compromise of their corporate Microsoft Entra account.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: A compromised Microsoft Entra account was used to gain access to Charter's internal systems, specifically the company's Salesforce environment.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Customer data, including PII, was collected and exfiltrated from the company's Salesforce instance after attackers gained access.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Data was likely exfiltrated from the Salesforce environment to attacker-controlled infrastructure using common web protocols to evade detection.

identities.name: Charter Communications
identities.description: A major U.S. telecommunications provider, operating as Spectrum, that suffered a data breach impacting 4.9 million customer accounts after an employee's Microsoft Entra account was compromised via a vishing attack.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled infrastructure used to receive the customer data exfiltrated from Charter's Salesforce environment.

indicators.name: Anomalous Microsoft Entra ID Sign-in
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Microsoft Entra ID Sign-in
id: 7b9c1d3a-f2e1-4b8c-9a0d-5e6f7a8b9c0d
status: experimental
description: Monitors for anomalous Microsoft Entra ID sign-in events, such as logins from unfamiliar locations or impossible travel scenarios, which could indicate a compromised account.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/charter-communications-data-breach-affects-49-million-accounts/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user travel.
  - VPN usage.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    properties.status.errorCode: 0
    properties.riskLevelDuringSignIn: high
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Monitors for anomalous Microsoft Entra ID sign-in events, such as logins from unfamiliar locations or impossible travel, which could indicate a compromised account as seen in the Charter breach.

indicators.name: Anomalous Salesforce Data Export
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Salesforce Data Export
id: 8c0d2e4b-a3f2-4c9d-a0b1-6f7a8b9c0d1e
status: experimental
description: Monitors for unusual data access patterns in Salesforce, such as a single user account accessing or exporting an abnormally large number of records.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/charter-communications-data-breach-affects-49-million-accounts/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative tasks or data migrations.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: salesforce
  category: cloud
detection:
  selection:
    event.name: 'ReportExported'
  timeframe: 1h
  condition: selection | count() > 10
indicators.description: Monitors for unusual data access patterns in Salesforce, such as a single user account exporting an abnormally large number of records, which could indicate data exfiltration.

indicators.name: PowerShell Data Export Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Data Export Activity
id: 9d1e3f5c-a4b3-4d0e-b1c2-7a8b9c0d1e2f
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell commands used to export large amounts of data, which could be a sign of internal data staging for exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/charter-communications-data-breach-affects-49-million-accounts/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1048
  - attack.exfiltration
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate system administration scripts.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Export-Csv'
      - 'Out-File'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell commands like 'Export-Csv' or 'Out-File' which could be used for internal data staging prior to exfiltration.
